code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test04-APR-2021Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, and ketonuria, with low specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), but no evidence of infection. Low HbA1c with glucosuria and ketonuria may indicate recent acute metabolic disturbance (e.g., starvation, acute illness, or renal glycosuria) rather than chronic hyperglycemia. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for acute or chronic kidney disease and underlying metabolic derangements.
